task_completion.visual_evidence: Evidence of an image editor (like MS Paint) open with a 32x32 pixel canvas, or the properties of the created image showing 32x32 dimensions.

task_completion.description: Save the image to the Custom Waypoint Symbols directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file explorer showing the file '001.bmp' saved in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\My Garmin\Custom Waypoint Symbols\'.

task_completion.description: Create a waypoint named 'Sensor-Alpha' at the specified coordinates
task_completion.visual_evidence: A waypoint properties window or list in the Garmin application showing the name 'Sensor-Alpha' and coordinates 42.445, -71.095 (or N 42.445, W 71.095).

task_completion.description: Assign the custom symbol to the waypoint
task_completion.visual_evidence: The waypoint properties or map view showing the custom symbol (the created BMP image) assigned to the 'Sensor-Alpha' waypoint instead of a default icon.

task_completion.description: Export the waypoint data to a GPX file
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export/save dialog or file explorer showing 'sensor_network.gpx' being saved to or existing in 'C:\workspace\output\'.

integrity.description: The agent used an actual image editor to create the BMP file
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show an image editing application (like MS Paint) being used, rather than a script or terminal command generating the file.

integrity.description: The agent used Garmin software to create the waypoint and assign the symbol
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a Garmin application (like BaseCamp) open and being interacted with to create the waypoint.

integrity.description: The agent exported the GPX file via the application rather than typing it manually
integrity.visual_evidence: The GPX file should be generated using the application's export functionality, not by writing XML/GPX code in a text editor.
